latestEntry.talkTitle: The Need for More Multidisciplinary Skills
latestEntry.notes: Week 7 Day 7 of Talk 5 (Week 1 of 1, Single-Week Talk - FINAL DAY). Reflection & Integration focus: Synthesis of entire week into personal action plan. Week in Review: One Big Idea (personal multidisciplinary education system with 3 components: latticework, simulator practice, checklists), Three Models (man-with-a-hammer syndrome, aircraft simulator analogy, two-track analysis). Integration Exercise: 4-step process to design personal system (audit current latticework, design simulator routine, create decision checklist, commit to one change). Core frameworks: Aircraft Simulator Analogy (practice-based fluency), Two-Track Analysis (rational + subconscious), Fundamental Four-Discipline Combination (math/physics/chemistry/engineering), 6-Element Pilot Training Model, Principle of Economy. 5 actionable takeaways: build latticework, create simulator routine, use two-track analysis, apply inversion to career, audit for man-with-a-hammer bias. 5 case snapshots: LTCM collapse (unidisciplinary failure), Quibi (ignored psychology/economics), product manager metrics-only thinking (ignored psychology), Berkshire's See's Candies (multidisciplinary success), founder ignored incentives (psychology missed). Mental model drill on Latticework integration with Day 7 prompts: adding ONE model to permanent latticework, reflecting on thinking changes. 3 reflection questions: audit of recent decision for multidisciplinary thinking, environment/culture incentives, latticework building (fundamental four-discipline assessment). Personal commitment: 15-min man-with-a-hammer audit on most important recent decision. Commitment for next week: Read Talk 6 (Charitable Foundations) with multidisciplinary lenses (economics/psychology/governance). Personal Latticework Update: Added 9 models this week (man-with-a-hammer, aircraft simulator, two-track analysis, fundamental four-discipline, practice-based fluency, incentive-caused bias, use-it-or-lose-it, availability tendency, twaddle tendency), reinforced 5 models (inversion, latticework, circle of competence, checklist thinking, margin of safety). Total models: 25+. Week 7 complete. Next: Week 8 Day 1 - Talk 6 (Charitable Foundations) begins May 10. Message delivered successfully.
